author: misilva73
content: I was thinking a bit more about how the data floor impact tx and block accounting and I think the following formulas would make more sense:

author: misilva73
content:
Transaction accounting (unchanged):
```python
tx_gas_used_before_refund = tx.gas - tx_output.gas_left - tx_output.state_gas_reservoir
tx_gas_refund = min(tx_gas_used_before_refund // 5, tx_output.refund_counter)
tx_gas_used_after_refund = tx_gas_used_before_refund - tx_gas_refund
tx_gas_used = max(tx_gas_used_after_refund, calldata_floor_gas_cost) 
```

author: misilva73
content:
Block accounting (new formula):
```python
tx_state_gas = intrinsic_state_gas + tx_output.execution_state_gas_used
tx_regular_gas = tx_gas_used - tx_state_gas

block_output.block_regular_gas_used += tx_regular_gas
block_output.block_state_gas_used += tx_state_gas
```

author: misilva73
content: Before, if we summed `tx_state_gas + tx_regular_gas`, we would not necessarily get `tx_gas_used`, so block accounting and tx accounting would not match

author: misilva73
content: I still need to fix the `tx_gas_refund`, as it should not be counted towards the block limit

author: garyrong
content: It looks good to me at first glance, but I still need to double-check the corner cases tomorrow. In particular, I want to verify that the formula still holds when gas is moved from the regular reservoir to the state reservoir, e.g. via charge-from-regular followed by refund-to-state-reservoir.

author: misilva73
content:
To integrate this with 7778, we would need to do:

```python
tx_state_gas = intrinsic_state_gas + tx_output.execution_state_gas_used

if calldata_floor_gas_cost >= tx_gas_used_after_refund:
     tx_regular_gas = tx_gas_used - tx_state_gas
else:
     tx_regular_gas = tx_gas_used - tx_state_gas + tx_gas_refund

block_output.block_regular_gas_used += tx_regular_gas
block_output.block_state_gas_used += tx_state_gas
```

author: misilva73
content: <@755913785620824175> , would love to hear your thoughts on this
